summary: Julia describes a low-energy day where she translated a bash script for device mapper setup into Go. She also experimented with the vim-go plugin and found three useful features: auto-formatting, building, and go-to-definition. The go-to-definition feature stood out as genuinely useful compared to her usual approach of grepping through code.
key_points: Translating bash scripts to Go can be a productive low-energy task that still moves a project forward
The vim-go plugin's go-to-definition feature was a significant improvement over manually grepping for function definitions
Auto-formatting and build shortcuts feel marginal when you can just switch to a terminal, but navigation features provide real value
money_quotes: The format and builder things are fine (it doesn't seem that much better to me than just switching to a terminal to do the same thing), but the go to definition thing is GREAT.
Usually I do "go to definition" with a lot of grep so this was a lot faster.
On Wednesday I was feeling tired so I didn't do too much.
key_insight: Editor features that improve code navigation (like go-to-definition) provide more value than ones that merely shortcut terminal commands you already know.
